stem: Which of the following BEST describes the security level of assets regarding integrity?
core_concern: CIA triad — Integrity definition
syllabus_topic: Assets and Their Corresponding Protection Level

stem: Which one of the following is an adequate alternative to describe how security testing can confirm that confidentiality of sensitive information has proper safeguards?
core_concern: CIA triad — Confidentiality testing

stem: Which of the following option BEST describes a security audit?
core_concern: Security audit definition vs. pentest vs. vulnerability scan

stem: Which one of the following options describes Zero Trust?
core_concern: Zero Trust — core principle (never trust, always verify)

stem: Which of the following would you include to verify that the concept of Zero Trust has been correctly implemented?
core_concern: Zero Trust — implementation verification test conditions

stem: When using open-source software, which of the following is NOT a critical factor to consider when addressing security concerns?
core_concern: OSS security risk factors — what matters vs. what does not
syllabus_topic: The Concept of OSS and its Impact on Security Testing

stem: A bank has subcontracted development of new features for its customers portal. The bank calls you to plan and perform security tests on a pre-production environment before deployment. Which of the following best describes how you would approach this task?
core_concern: Selecting appropriate test type (black/grey/white-box) given access constraints
syllabus_topic: Black-Box Testing, White-Box Testing, and Grey-Box Testing

stem: As the security test engineer you are tasked with defining the security test techniques that must be applied as static security testing. What would be your approach?
core_concern: Static security testing — correct ordering and scope (requirements → design → code)

stem: Given a password reset requirement, which of the following is the minimum list of test conditions to adequately test the functional security covered by this requirement?
core_concern: Test condition derivation for authentication/password reset flows

stem: You are responsible for IAM to manage and maintain user accounts and rights. Two newcomers and one person changed department. What security test techniques should you plan?
core_concern: IAM testing — recertification and role/privilege verification after changes

stem: Which of the following CORRECTLY describes security test techniques for authentication mechanism?
core_concern: Authentication testing — factory defaults, password strength

stem: Which of the following statements BEST describes how to test data protection controls?
core_concern: Data protection testing — encryption, access controls, data masking

stem: You are asked to explain procedures for evaluating system hardening as a typical example of a protective technology. What procedure might you follow?
core_concern: Protective technology testing — system hardening evaluation procedure

stem: You are responsible for all aspects of the security process. You use high-level tests as a basis for manual tests executed from an external vendor's perspective. Which security test task can be done in parallel with this?
core_concern: ISTQB Security Test Process — task parallelism and sequencing

stem: Which of the following is a main characteristic of an effective security test environment?
core_concern: Security test environment — isolation and production likeness

stem: During component testing, which compiler warning would trigger the security tester most?
core_concern: Component-level security testing — compiler warnings priority (security vs. functional)
syllabus_topic: Security Test Design at Component Test Level

stem: Component A and B communicate via REST API. Which ONE of the following is an example of a security test performed at the component integration level?
core_concern: Component integration security testing — inter-component communication security
syllabus_topic: Security Test Design at the Component Integration Level

stem: Which one of the following BEST describes the correct procedure for implementation of end-to-end security testing to test the system's handling of unsuccessful login attempts?
core_concern: System-level security testing — login lockout and account protection E2E
syllabus_topic: Security Testing in System Testing and Acceptance Testing

stem: No explicit security requirements exist for a new banking application. You select test cases from standards and best practices. Which three statements guide you best for selecting test cases?
core_concern: Selecting test cases from standards when requirements are absent — norms vs. best practices validity

stem: You are asked to do a black-box pentest on a new banking core system. How can you leverage standards for your task?
core_concern: CWE/CVE/CVSS standards for pentest — prioritization and test case derivation

stem: When you use test oracles for an application from standards and best practices, what do you have to consider?
core_concern: Test oracles from standards — context-dependency and applicability limits
syllabus_topic: Test Oracles Extracted from Standards and Best Practices

stem: Best practices and standards deliver artefacts which can be used effectively for security testing. Which combinations of artefact and activity map correctly?
core_concern: Standards artefacts — correct mapping of artefact type to use case
syllabus_topic: Advantages and Disadvantages of Leveraging Security Testing Standards and Best Practices

stem: You are hired to compromise at least one system in an automotive company's industrial network. Which TWO options best leverage the organizational context?
core_concern: Organizational context exploitation in pentesting — social engineering and supply chain vectors
syllabus_topic: Analyze a given organizational context for security testing

stem: Your company develops communication devices for the aviation industry. What ONE aspect do you BEST need to consider for security testing before market launch?
core_concern: Regulated sector security testing — compliance requirements drive test scope
syllabus_topic: The Impact of Governmental Regulations on Security Regulations

stem: During security testing of a core system you find suspicious files not created by testers or applications. Choose the option BEST describing how you would proceed.
core_concern: Incident response during testing — follow policy, escalate to CISO

stem: Certain steps are common for almost every attack. These steps can be defined as:
core_concern: Attack anatomy / kill chain — reconnaissance, exploitation, persistence sequence
syllabus_topic: Addressing Security Risks in Test Design

stem: Which ONE of the following statements describes BEST how security testing should be implemented in the development lifecycle?
core_concern: Security testing in SDLC — each dev activity has a corresponding security test activity

stem: Which TWO of the following statements BEST describe the impact of a software development model on security testing?
core_concern: SDLC model impact — Agile/DevOps vs. Waterfall on security testing integration

stem: Which of the following four statements is true for security testing within the context of maintenance testing?
core_concern: Maintenance security testing — regression for new vulnerabilities introduced by change
syllabus_topic: Security Testing in System Testing and Acceptance Testing

stem: Which of the following describes BEST why you should analyse security testing results?
core_concern: Purpose of security test result analysis — threat understanding drives scope

stem: Somebody does a penetration test covering OWASP Top-10 vulnerabilities. Which reasoning on accepting or rejecting the test report is correct?
core_concern: Evaluating pentest reports against standards — acceptance criteria alignment
syllabus_topic: Mandatory Application of Standards and Best Practices

stem: To leverage security testing to the highest level of efficiency and effectiveness it must:
core_concern: Security testing integration into overall security process — not a standalone activity
syllabus_topic: Analyze a given organizational context for security testing

stem: Typical dimensions a security test engineer can use for enhancing ISMS scope — correct pairing of actions and goals?
core_concern: ISMS scope enhancement — mapping test engineer actions to ISMS goals
syllabus_topic: Analyze a given organizational context for security testing

stem: How can security testing improve measurability within an ISMS?
core_concern: ISMS measurability — security testing as objective input to PDCA Check step
syllabus_topic: Analyze a given organizational context for security testing

stem: Security test reports should be handled with high confidentiality. What type of data motivates this classification?
core_concern: Security test report handling — CVE/vulnerability data sensitivity

stem: You executed security test cases as part of a pentest and identified a possible vulnerability with dramatic business impact. What to do before you directly motivate its mitigation?
core_concern: Vulnerability management — demarcation and risk adjustment before mitigation

stem: You identified a CVSS 9.8 vulnerability confirmed exploitable on production with strong negative business impact. The application is business-critical. What is your mitigation recommendation?
core_concern: CVSS-based risk mitigation — compensating controls vs. direct fix on critical systems

stem: In a CI/CD environment a new pipeline is being put together. Which one of the following would you recommend being the first triggered step?
core_concern: CI/CD security pipeline ordering — SAST runs first (no running app needed)

stem: Which of the following test scanners & methods are scanning the application under test during run time?
core_concern: Static vs. dynamic scanning — DAST operates at runtime

stem: Which test objects can be scanned by static testing tools?
core_concern: Static testing scope — what can be analysed without execution (config files, source)
